text:
Market today: Iran strike deal plus oil back above $70 is the Monday mood-setter. Geopolitical relief usually pumps risk-on briefly, but rising oil is a cost headwind for tech/semis — net effect is muted to slightly positive for your names today.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one demanding attention — it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF that decays in choppy markets and your loss is already meaningful; on margin that hurt is doubled. Stop at $200 gives more room but watch closely. SMH (-0.69%) is mild but it's the same semi theme — if chips stay under pressure SOXL bleeds faster. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; tiny moves feel bigger on margin, size it small and manage it by hand.

Watch: 1) Semi sector tone this week — any macro wobble hits SOXL hardest and fastest. 2) Oil trajectory — sustained rise above $70 quietly pressures tech margins and risk appetite.

Bottom line: Your real risk today is SOXL decay eating you alive on margin — decide if that position size still makes sense.
